acceptance_criteria.given: commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode is invoked for active task-id T
acceptance_criteria.when: agent appends a new cycle block to a spec file at docs/dev/specs/spec-*.md
acceptance_criteria.then: the spec file contains the literal string <!-- spec-continuation-of: T --> where T is the resolved task-id value (not a placeholder)

check.note: Replace T with the actual task-id value used in the spec-update run

acceptance_criteria.given: a spec file at docs/dev/specs/spec-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.md produced by spec-update continuation run for task-id T
acceptance_criteria.when: Stage 3 content predicate runs grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->' <spec_path>
acceptance_criteria.then: grep exits 0 and returns the spec path (content predicate passes; basename pattern and mtime window are enforced by commit.md separately and not tested here)

check.note: Replace T with resolved task-id. File must exist and be named spec-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.md for full Stage 3 eligibility.

acceptance_criteria.given: a spec file produced by spec-update continuation run for task-id T
acceptance_criteria.when: checking for literal placeholder forms of the marker
acceptance_criteria.then: the spec file does NOT contain the literal strings <!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} --> or <!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> -->

check.note: Both greps must exit 1 (no match). Exit 0 means a literal placeholder was written — FAIL.

acceptance_criteria.then: spec-continuation-of text appears inside ## Continuation-spec mode section only; ## Temp-note mode section contains no spec-continuation-of text

check.expected: all matching line numbers fall between the ## Continuation-spec mode heading and the ## Temp-note mode heading
check.note: QA verifier must check line numbers against section boundaries

acceptance_criteria.given: a spec file already containing <!-- spec-continuation-of: T --> for task-id T
acceptance_criteria.when: spec-update continuation mode is invoked again for the same task-id T on the same spec
acceptance_criteria.then: the spec file contains exactly one occurrence of <!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->

check.note: Replace T with resolved task-id. count > 1 means the idempotency check is missing — FAIL.
